page: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.16xlarge is an ARM64-based server powered by AWS Graviton processors using the Neoverse-V1 microarchitecture. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at 2.6 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is delivered via a 200 Gbps baseline bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities and high memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench multi-core testing and software compilation tasks, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Memory bandwidth tests show strong read and write speeds, reaching 276.16 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. However, single-core performance remains in the average range across stress-ng and Geekbench tests. LLM text generation performance varies, showing weak results for small 135M models but strong results for medium 7B models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the hpc7g.16xlarge offers high compute density and memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The primary tradeoffs include the complete absence of local storage and GPU acceleration, as well as a relatively low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This hardware profile is suited for highly parallelized compute-bound workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and memory-bandwidth-sensitive scientific simulations, rather than memory-intensive databases or single-threaded applications.
description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 architecture running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing 2.0 GB of memory per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core execution and memory bandwidth, alongside strong software compilation and static web serving capabilities, though single-core performance is average. This profile is optimized for parallelized high-performance computing, software compilation, and memory-bandwidth-sensitive workloads, presenting a tradeoff of low memory-to-core density and no local disk storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services hpc7g.16xlarge is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-focused server with 64 dedicated cores, high memory bandwidth, and 200 Gbps networking for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB system memory with high-throughput memory bandwidth
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core CPU and software compilation benchmark scores
No local storage or GPU hardware included
Suited for parallel scientific computing and software compilation
